metadata.contractTitle: Statement of Work - ITSM Managed Infrastructure & Server Maintenance Services
metadata.renewalTerms: May be renewed or extended by mutual written agreement between the parties
metadata.pricingFull: Managed Services - Fixed Monthly Fee of USD 25,000 per month, billed monthly in arrears
metadata.contractValue: USD 300,000 (Annual Contract Value for 12-month initial term)
metadata.emLevel: EM3 – Delivery+: Supplier owns delivery scheduling, milestone tracking, and meeting milestones per the Service Transition and Delivery Plan (Annexure 1). Supplier has SLA/KPI commitments (response and resolution targets) but without financial penalties beyond capped service credits (max 5% monthly, 10% annual). Supplier does not own fixed-price budget or scope ownership (changes require change requests). This is EM3 because the model includes delivery ownership, SLA commitments, and milestone adherence, but the commercial model is fixed monthly fee (not fixed-price project), SLA remedies are lenient service credits with caps and broad exclusions (not true penalties), and scope changes are handled through change requests rather than supplier-owned scope management.
metadata.serviceCreditCaps: Maximum service credit in any calendar month shall not exceed 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee. Maximum cumulative service credits during the initial term shall not exceed 10% of Annual Contract Value (USD 30,000).
metadata.terminationTerms: May be terminated earlier in accordance with the Master Services Agreement dated 01-Jun-26. Supplier entitled to payment for all Services performed up to effective date of termination, including accepted Services, work in progress, and any investments or third-party costs incurred.
metadata.securityTerms: Supplier personnel shall use Customer-approved access methods, follow least privilege principles, and comply with Customer security instructions provided in writing. Supplier shall treat Customer information, credentials, infrastructure details, and operational data as confidential.
metadata.slaTerms: P1 Critical: 15 minutes initial response, 4 hours resolution/workaround. P2 High: 30 minutes initial response, 8 hours resolution/workaround. P3 Medium: 4 business hours initial response, 24 business hours resolution/workaround. P4 Low: 1 business day initial response, 3 business days resolution/workaround. Resolution targets assume required access, approvals, information, and third-party support availability. Monthly SLA achievement below 98% triggers service credits (2%-5% of monthly fee based on achievement level).
metadata.scopeChangeTerms: Changes in scope, deliverables, service levels, server inventory, support hours, resource model, dependencies, tools, or fees only through written change request or written amendment signed by both parties. Requirements not expressly listed as In Scope shall be treated as change requests.
metadata.chargeableEvents: New server build or provisioning beyond agreed baseline, major upgrade projects, cloud migration activities, disaster recovery setup or DR drill ownership, security remediation projects, new application support or database administration ownership, increase beyond agreed server inventory baseline (150 servers), onsite travel or out-of-pocket expenses if pre-approved - all handled as Time and Material or Change Request.
metadata.exitObligations: Supplier entitled to payment for all Services performed up to effective date of termination, including accepted Services, work in progress, and any investments or third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of Customer.
metadata.executionEvidence: Signature blocks present for both parties (Yen Corp. Ltd. and CTIND Pvt. Ltd.) but signature lines are blank/unfilled in the uploaded document. Execution evidence incomplete - manual validation required to confirm authorized signatories, dates, and actual execution.

rows.measurement: Not applicable
rows.clause: This SOW shall be governed by the terms and conditions contained in the Master Services Agreement dated 01-Jun-26. In case of conflict between this SOW and the Agreement, the Agreement shall prevail unless expressly stated otherwise in this SOW.
rows.valueAtRisk: Not quantifiable - governance risk
rows.customerDependency: Customer must ensure MSA terms are acceptable and do not contain unfavorable liability, indemnity, termination, IP, or payment terms
rows.exclusions: Not applicable
rows.recommendedAction: Obtain and review the Master Services Agreement dated 01-Jun-26 to validate liability caps, indemnity scope, termination rights, IP ownership, payment terms, dispute resolution, and governing law before accepting this SOW.
rows.evidence: This SOW shall be governed by the terms and conditions contained in the Master Services Agreement dated 01-Jun-26. In case of conflict between this SOW and the Agreement, the Agreement shall prevail unless expressly stated otherwise in this SOW.
rows.reason: The MSA is the governing agreement but is not uploaded. Material commercial, legal, liability, indemnity, termination, IP, and dispute terms in the MSA will control this engagement. Without reviewing the MSA, Customer cannot assess total risk exposure, liability caps, termination rights, or unfavorable terms that may override this SOW.

rows.measurement: Time from incident logging/alert to Supplier's initial response acknowledgment
rows.clause: P1 Critical: Complete outage of critical production service or multiple critical servers unavailable. Initial Response Target: 15 minutes. Resolution / Workaround Target: 4 hours.
rows.valueAtRisk: Service credit exposure: 2%-5% of monthly fee (USD 500 - USD 1,250) per month if SLA achievement falls below 98%
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide complete server inventory, criticality classification, monitoring thresholds, timely access, approvals, and third-party vendor support
rows.exclusions: Service credits do not apply where failure is caused by Customer, third-party providers, tool unavailability, pending approvals, scheduled maintenance, force majeure, security incidents caused by Customer systems, or infrastructure elements outside Supplier's control
rows.remedy: Service credit per Section 13: 2%-5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee based on monthly SLA achievement
rows.cap: Maximum 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee per month (USD 1,250); maximum 10% of Annual Contract Value cumulative (USD 30,000)
rows.recommendedAction: Validate that 15-minute P1 response target is achievable 24x7 given Supplier's staffing model, time zones, and escalation procedures. Clarify measurement methodology (ticket timestamp vs. acknowledgment vs. technical engagement) and ensure monitoring/alerting tools provide real-time notification to Supplier.
rows.evidence: P1 Critical: Complete outage of critical production service or multiple critical servers unavailable. Initial Response Target: 15 minutes.
rows.reason: 15-minute P1 response target is aggressive for 24x7 managed services and requires robust on-call staffing, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to meet this target repeatedly could trigger service credits, but broad exclusions and caps limit Customer's remedy. Measurement methodology is not explicitly defined.

rows.measurement: Time from incident logging to resolution or workaround, assuming required access, approvals, information, and third-party support availability
rows.clause: P1 Critical: Resolution / Workaround Target: 4 hours. Resolution targets assume that Supplier has required access, approvals, information, and third-party/vendor support availability. Where final resolution depends on Customer, third-party vendors, hardware replacement, telecom/network provider, cloud provider, or application vendor, Supplier shall be responsible for coordination and commercially reasonable support, not guaranteed resolution.
rows.valueAtRisk: Service credit exposure: 2%-5% of monthly fee (USD 500 - USD 1,250) per month if SLA achievement falls below 98%. Potential business impact from extended P1 outages not covered by service credits.
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide required access, approvals, information, third-party/vendor support availability, hardware replacement, telecom/network provider support, cloud provider support, and application vendor support
rows.exclusions: Where final resolution depends on Customer, third-party vendors, hardware replacement, telecom/network provider, cloud provider, or application vendor, Supplier responsible for coordination only, not guaranteed resolution. Service credits excluded for Customer-caused failures, third-party failures, tool unavailability, pending approvals, scheduled maintenance, force majeure, or infrastructure outside Supplier's control.
rows.remedy: Service credit per Section 13: 2%-5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee based on monthly SLA achievement
rows.cap: Maximum 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee per month (USD 1,250); maximum 10% of Annual Contract Value cumulative (USD 30,000)
rows.owner: Supplier Incident Management Team / Customer Infrastructure Owner
rows.recommendedAction: Negotiate tighter definition of 'resolution' vs. 'workaround' and clarify Supplier's accountability when third-party dependencies exist. Establish clear escalation paths, vendor support SLAs, and Customer approval turnaround commitments. Consider higher service credit percentages or uncapped credits for repeated P1 SLA failures. Require root cause analysis and corrective action for all P1 SLA breaches.
rows.evidence: Resolution targets assume that Supplier has required access, approvals, information, and third-party/vendor support availability. Where final resolution depends on Customer, third-party vendors, hardware replacement, telecom/network provider, cloud provider, or application vendor, Supplier shall be responsible for coordination and commercially reasonable support, not guaranteed resolution.
rows.reason: 4-hour P1 resolution target is ambitious and heavily dependent on Customer and third-party support. The broad exclusions and 'coordination only' language significantly limit Supplier's accountability for P1 resolution. Service credit caps (5% monthly, 10% annual) are low relative to potential business impact of extended critical outages. Customer has limited financial remedy for repeated P1 failures.

rows.measurement: Time from incident logging/alert to Supplier's initial response acknowledgment
rows.clause: P2 High: Major degradation affecting significant users or business process. Initial Response Target: 30 minutes. Resolution / Workaround Target: 8 hours.
rows.valueAtRisk: Service credit exposure: 2%-5% of monthly fee (USD 500 - USD 1,250) per month if SLA achievement falls below 98%
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide complete server inventory, criticality classification, monitoring thresholds, timely access, approvals, and third-party vendor support
rows.exclusions: Service credits do not apply where failure is caused by Customer, third-party providers, tool unavailability, pending approvals, scheduled maintenance, force majeure, security incidents caused by Customer systems, or infrastructure elements outside Supplier's control
rows.remedy: Service credit per Section 13: 2%-5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee based on monthly SLA achievement
rows.cap: Maximum 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee per month (USD 1,250); maximum 10% of Annual Contract Value cumulative (USD 30,000)
rows.recommendedAction: Validate 30-minute P2 response target is achievable during business hours and after-hours. Clarify measurement methodology and ensure monitoring/alerting tools provide timely notification.
rows.evidence: P2 High: Major degradation affecting significant users or business process. Initial Response Target: 30 minutes.
rows.reason: 30-minute P2 response target is reasonable but requires effective monitoring and on-call coverage. Broad exclusions and service credit caps limit Customer's remedy for repeated failures.

rows.measurement: Time from incident logging to resolution or workaround, assuming required access, approvals, information, and third-party support availability
rows.clause: P2 High: Resolution / Workaround Target: 8 hours. Resolution targets assume that Supplier has required access, approvals, information, and third-party/vendor support availability.
rows.valueAtRisk: Service credit exposure: 2%-5% of monthly fee (USD 500 - USD 1,250) per month if SLA achievement falls below 98%
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide required access, approvals, information, and third-party/vendor support availability
rows.exclusions: Where final resolution depends on Customer, third-party vendors, hardware, telecom, cloud, or application vendor, Supplier responsible for coordination only. Service credits excluded for Customer-caused failures, third-party failures, tool unavailability, pending approvals, scheduled maintenance, force majeure, or infrastructure outside Supplier's control.
rows.remedy: Service credit per Section 13: 2%-5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee based on monthly SLA achievement
rows.cap: Maximum 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee per month (USD 1,250); maximum 10% of Annual Contract Value cumulative (USD 30,000)
rows.owner: Supplier Incident Management Team / Customer Infrastructure Owner
rows.recommendedAction: Clarify Supplier's accountability when third-party dependencies exist. Establish clear escalation paths and Customer approval turnaround commitments for P2 incidents.
rows.evidence: P2 High: Resolution / Workaround Target: 8 hours. Resolution targets assume that Supplier has required access, approvals, information, and third-party/vendor support availability.
rows.reason: 8-hour P2 resolution target is dependent on Customer and third-party support. Broad exclusions limit Supplier's accountability. Service credit caps provide limited financial remedy.

rows.measurement: Time from incident logging to Supplier's initial response acknowledgment during business hours
rows.clause: P3 Medium: Limited impact issue or non-critical server/service issue. Initial Response Target: 4 business hours. Resolution / Workaround Target: 24 business hours.
rows.valueAtRisk: Service credit exposure: 2%-5% of monthly fee (USD 500 - USD 1,250) per month if SLA achievement falls below 98%
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide server inventory, monitoring thresholds, access, and approvals
rows.exclusions: Service credits do not apply where failure is caused by Customer, third-party providers, tool unavailability, pending approvals, scheduled maintenance, force majeure, security incidents caused by Customer systems, or infrastructure elements outside Supplier's control
rows.remedy: Service credit per Section 13: 2%-5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee based on monthly SLA achievement
rows.cap: Maximum 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee per month (USD 1,250); maximum 10% of Annual Contract Value cumulative (USD 30,000)
rows.recommendedAction: Validate business hours definition (time zone, days, holidays) and ensure P3 response target aligns with Customer's operational expectations.
rows.evidence: P3 Medium: Limited impact issue or non-critical server/service issue. Initial Response Target: 4 business hours.
rows.reason: 4 business hours P3 response target is reasonable for medium-priority issues. Broad exclusions and service credit caps limit Customer's remedy.

rows.measurement: Time from incident logging to resolution or workaround during business hours, assuming required access, approvals, and support availability
rows.clause: P3 Medium: Resolution / Workaround Target: 24 business hours. Resolution targets assume that Supplier has required access, approvals, information, and third-party/vendor support availability.
rows.valueAtRisk: Service credit exposure: 2%-5% of monthly fee (USD 500 - USD 1,250) per month if SLA achievement falls below 98%
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide required access, approvals, information, and third-party/vendor support availability
rows.exclusions: Where final resolution depends on Customer, third-party vendors, hardware, telecom, cloud, or application vendor, Supplier responsible for coordination only. Service credits excluded for Customer-caused failures, third-party failures, tool unavailability, pending approvals, scheduled maintenance, force majeure, or infrastructure outside Supplier's control.
rows.remedy: Service credit per Section 13: 2%-5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee based on monthly SLA achievement
rows.cap: Maximum 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee per month (USD 1,250); maximum 10% of Annual Contract Value cumulative (USD 30,000)
rows.recommendedAction: Validate business hours definition and ensure P3 resolution target aligns with Customer's operational expectations.
rows.evidence: P3 Medium: Resolution / Workaround Target: 24 business hours.
rows.reason: 24 business hours P3 resolution target is reasonable for medium-priority issues. Broad exclusions limit Supplier's accountability.

rows.measurement: Time from service request logging to Supplier's initial response acknowledgment during business hours
rows.clause: P4 Low: Standard service request, query, or low impact issue. Initial Response Target: 1 business day. Resolution / Workaround Target: 3 business days.
rows.valueAtRisk: Service credit exposure: 2%-5% of monthly fee (USD 500 - USD 1,250) per month if SLA achievement falls below 98%
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide clear service request details and approvals
rows.exclusions: Service credits do not apply where failure is caused by Customer, third-party providers, tool unavailability, pending approvals, scheduled maintenance, force majeure, security incidents caused by Customer systems, or infrastructure elements outside Supplier's control
rows.remedy: Service credit per Section 13: 2%-5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee based on monthly SLA achievement
rows.cap: Maximum 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee per month (USD 1,250); maximum 10% of Annual Contract Value cumulative (USD 30,000)
rows.recommendedAction: Validate business day definition and ensure P4 response target aligns with Customer's service request expectations.
rows.evidence: P4 Low: Standard service request, query, or low impact issue. Initial Response Target: 1 business day.
rows.reason: 1 business day P4 response target is reasonable for low-priority service requests. Broad exclusions and service credit caps limit Customer's remedy.

rows.measurement: Time from service request logging to fulfillment during business hours, assuming required approvals and access
rows.clause: P4 Low: Resolution / Workaround Target: 3 business days. Resolution targets assume that Supplier has required access, approvals, information, and third-party/vendor support availability.
rows.valueAtRisk: Service credit exposure: 2%-5% of monthly fee (USD 500 - USD 1,250) per month if SLA achievement falls below 98%
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide required approvals and access for service request fulfillment
rows.exclusions: Where final resolution depends on Customer, third-party vendors, hardware, telecom, cloud, or application vendor, Supplier responsible for coordination only. Service credits excluded for Customer-caused failures, third-party failures, tool unavailability, pending approvals, scheduled maintenance, force majeure, or infrastructure outside Supplier's control.
rows.remedy: Service credit per Section 13: 2%-5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee based on monthly SLA achievement
rows.cap: Maximum 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee per month (USD 1,250); maximum 10% of Annual Contract Value cumulative (USD 30,000)
rows.recommendedAction: Validate business day definition and ensure P4 resolution target aligns with Customer's service request expectations.
rows.evidence: P4 Low: Resolution / Workaround Target: 3 business days.
rows.reason: 3 business days P4 resolution target is reasonable for low-priority service requests. Broad exclusions limit Supplier's accountability.

rows.section: Section 13 - Lenient Service Credit / Penalty Clause
rows.measurement: Monthly SLA achievement percentage across all priority levels
rows.clause: Service credits shall be Customer's sole and exclusive remedy for failure to meet applicable service levels. Maximum service credit in any calendar month shall not exceed 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee. Maximum cumulative service credits during the initial term shall not exceed 10% of Annual Contract Value.
rows.valueAtRisk: Maximum service credit exposure: USD 1,250 per month (5% of USD 25,000); USD 30,000 cumulative (10% of USD 300,000 annual value). Potential business impact from SLA failures not covered by service credits.
rows.customerDependency: Customer must track SLA performance, raise service credit claims within invoice dispute window (15 business days), and accept service credits as sole remedy
rows.exclusions: Service credits shall not apply where failure is caused by Customer, third-party providers, tool unavailability, pending approvals, scheduled maintenance, force majeure, security incidents caused by Customer systems, or infrastructure elements outside Supplier's control. Supplier shall not be liable for consequential, indirect, punitive, exemplary, or loss-of-business damages arising from SLA failure.
rows.remedy: Service credit: 2% of Monthly Managed Services Fee (95%-97.99% achievement), 3% (92%-94.99%), 4% (90%-91.99%), 5% (below 90%). Applied against future invoices, not payable as cash refund.
rows.cap: Maximum 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee per month (USD 1,250); maximum 10% of Annual Contract Value cumulative (USD 30,000)
rows.recommendedAction: Negotiate higher service credit percentages (e.g., 10%-20% for severe or repeated SLA failures) and remove or raise the cumulative cap (10% annual cap is low for a 12-month engagement). Negotiate tighter exclusions (e.g., require Supplier to prove Customer/third-party causation). Add provisions for termination rights or price reduction if SLA achievement falls below threshold (e.g., below 90%) for consecutive months. Require root cause analysis and corrective action plans for all SLA breaches. Consider adding liquidated damages for material or repeated P1 SLA failures.
rows.evidence: Service credits shall be Customer's sole and exclusive remedy for failure to meet applicable service levels. Maximum service credit in any calendar month shall not exceed 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee. Maximum cumulative service credits during the initial term shall not exceed 10% of Annual Contract Value.
rows.reason: Service credit caps (5% monthly, 10% annual) are very low relative to contract value (USD 300,000) and potential business impact of SLA failures. Broad exclusions significantly limit Customer's ability to claim service credits. 'Sole and exclusive remedy' language prevents Customer from seeking other damages or termination for SLA failures. Customer has limited financial leverage to enforce SLA performance.

rows.measurement: Not applicable
rows.clause: Provide timely access to servers, monitoring tools, ticketing tools, VPN, privileged access management tools, backup tools, and cloud consoles as required. Provide licenses, subscriptions, third-party vendor support, and tool availability necessary to perform the Services.
rows.valueAtRisk: Delays in service delivery, SLA exclusions, potential change requests or additional fees if Customer fails to provide required access, tools, or licenses
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide complete and timely access to servers, monitoring tools, ticketing tools, VPN, privileged access management tools, backup tools, cloud consoles, licenses, subscriptions, third-party vendor support, and tool availability
rows.exclusions: Supplier not responsible for outages or delays caused by lack of access, incorrect access, pending approvals, unsupported systems, third-party failures, or infrastructure outside agreed scope
rows.recommendedAction: Document specific access requirements, tool licenses, and third-party support commitments in a detailed transition checklist with target dates. Establish clear escalation process and accountability if Customer fails to provide required access or tools. Clarify whether delays caused by Customer dependencies will result in SLA exclusions, timeline extensions, or additional fees.
rows.evidence: Provide timely access to servers, monitoring tools, ticketing tools, VPN, privileged access management tools, backup tools, and cloud consoles as required. Provide licenses, subscriptions, third-party vendor support, and tool availability necessary to perform the Services.
rows.reason: Customer has extensive dependencies to provide access, tools, licenses, and third-party support. Failure to meet these dependencies can delay service delivery, trigger SLA exclusions, and potentially result in change requests or additional fees. The contract does not specify consequences or remedies if Customer fails to meet dependencies.

rows.measurement: Not applicable
rows.clause: Provide timely approvals for changes, access, emergency workarounds, downtime, and remediation actions. Ensure Customer applications, network, telecom, hardware, and third-party systems are supported by appropriate owners or vendors.
rows.valueAtRisk: Delays in incident resolution, SLA exclusions, potential business impact if Customer approvals are delayed or third-party support is unavailable
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide timely approvals for changes, access, emergency workarounds, downtime, and remediation actions. Customer must ensure applications, network, telecom, hardware, and third-party systems are supported by appropriate owners or vendors.
rows.exclusions: Supplier not responsible for outages or delays caused by pending approvals, unsupported systems, third-party failures, or infrastructure outside agreed scope
rows.recommendedAction: Define specific approval turnaround time commitments for Customer (e.g., P1 approvals within 30 minutes, P2 within 2 hours). Establish clear escalation paths and decision authority for emergency situations. Document third-party vendor support SLAs and ensure they align with or exceed Supplier's SLA commitments. Clarify whether delays caused by Customer approval or third-party support issues will result in SLA exclusions or timeline extensions.
rows.evidence: Provide timely approvals for changes, access, emergency workarounds, downtime, and remediation actions. Ensure Customer applications, network, telecom, hardware, and third-party systems are supported by appropriate owners or vendors.
rows.reason: Customer approval and third-party support dependencies can significantly impact Supplier's ability to meet SLA targets, especially for P1 and P2 incidents. The contract does not define 'timely' or specify approval turnaround commitments. Delays in Customer approvals or third-party support can trigger SLA exclusions, limiting Customer's remedy.

rows.measurement: Number of servers in agreed server inventory
rows.clause: Services are based on a maximum baseline of 150 servers unless otherwise agreed in writing. Any changes to server count, support hours, scope, tooling, SLA, regulatory requirements, security obligations, or support model may impact timelines, resource model, and fees.
rows.valueAtRisk: Additional fees or change requests if server count exceeds 150; potential service delivery issues if server count grows without corresponding resource or fee adjustments
rows.customerDependency: Customer must maintain server inventory within 150-server baseline or request change request for additional servers
rows.exclusions: Increase beyond agreed server inventory baseline requires change request or revised monthly fee
rows.remedy: Change request or revised monthly fee per Section 12
rows.recommendedAction: Validate current server count and projected growth during the 12-month term. Negotiate a higher baseline (e.g., 200 servers) or a tiered pricing model that accommodates growth without requiring change requests. Establish a clear process and pricing model for adding servers (e.g., per-server monthly fee). Require Supplier to notify Customer when server count approaches baseline limit.
rows.evidence: Services are based on a maximum baseline of 150 servers unless otherwise agreed in writing.
rows.reason: 150-server baseline may be insufficient if Customer's infrastructure grows during the 12-month term. Exceeding the baseline will trigger change requests or additional fees, potentially causing delays or budget overruns. The contract does not specify the pricing model for additional servers.

rows.measurement: Time from receipt of monthly service report to Customer's written acceptance or non-conformance
rows.clause: Services are deemed accepted monthly if Supplier provides the monthly service report and Customer does not raise written material non-conformance within ten (10) business days. If Customer does not provide written non-conformance within such period, the monthly Services shall be deemed accepted.
rows.valueAtRisk: Automatic acceptance of Services and payment obligation if Customer fails to review and dispute within 10 business days; potential loss of service credit claims or dispute rights
rows.customerDependency: Customer must review monthly service reports within 10 business days and raise written non-conformance with supporting evidence
rows.exclusions: Customer may dispute only specific non-conforming Services with supporting evidence from agreed tools or records
rows.recommendedAction: Negotiate a longer acceptance period (e.g., 15-20 business days) to allow adequate time for Customer review, especially for complex monthly reports. Clarify that deemed acceptance does not waive Customer's right to claim service credits for SLA failures documented in the report. Establish a clear process for raising non-conformance and resolving disputes. Require Supplier to provide monthly reports in a standardized format with clear SLA performance data.
rows.evidence: Services are deemed accepted monthly if Supplier provides the monthly service report and Customer does not raise written material non-conformance within ten (10) business days.
rows.reason: 10 business days is a short period for Customer to review monthly service reports, validate SLA performance, and raise non-conformance. Deemed acceptance may limit Customer's ability to dispute Services or claim service credits after the acceptance period. The contract does not clarify whether deemed acceptance waives service credit claims.

rows.measurement: Time from invoice date to Customer's written dispute of invoice items
rows.clause: Invoices deemed accepted if disputed items are not raised within 15 business days.
rows.valueAtRisk: Automatic acceptance of invoices and payment obligation if Customer fails to dispute within 15 business days; potential loss of ability to dispute incorrect charges or claim service credits
rows.customerDependency: Customer must review invoices within 15 business days and raise written disputes for incorrect charges or service credit claims
rows.exclusions: Not stated in uploaded contract
rows.recommendedAction: Negotiate a longer invoice dispute window (e.g., 30 business days) to allow adequate time for Customer review and validation. Clarify that deemed acceptance of invoices does not waive Customer's right to claim service credits for SLA failures. Establish a clear process for raising invoice disputes and resolving billing issues. Require Supplier to provide detailed invoices with clear line items and supporting documentation.
rows.evidence: Invoices deemed accepted if disputed items are not raised within 15 business days.
rows.reason: 15 business days is a short period for Customer to review invoices, validate charges, and raise disputes. Deemed acceptance may limit Customer's ability to dispute incorrect charges or claim service credits after the dispute window. The contract does not clarify whether deemed acceptance waives service credit claims.

rows.measurement: Not applicable
rows.clause: Supplier shall retain ownership of Supplier Background IP. Subject to full payment of applicable fees, Supplier grants Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable right to use such Supplier Background IP solely as embedded in or necessary for use of the deliverables for Customer's internal business purposes.
rows.valueAtRisk: Limited license to Supplier Background IP; potential operational disruption if Supplier terminates license or if Customer needs to transfer or sublicense IP
rows.customerDependency: Customer must pay all applicable fees to maintain license to Supplier Background IP
rows.exclusions: License is non-exclusive and non-transferable; limited to Customer's internal business purposes
rows.recommendedAction: Negotiate a perpetual, irrevocable license to Supplier Background IP embedded in deliverables, not contingent on ongoing payment. Negotiate rights to transfer or sublicense IP to Customer's affiliates, successors, or outsourcing providers. Clarify what happens to the license upon termination (e.g., does Customer retain rights to use embedded IP?). Request a list of Supplier Background IP components to assess dependency and exit risk.
rows.evidence: Supplier shall retain ownership of Supplier Background IP. Subject to full payment of applicable fees, Supplier grants Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable right to use such Supplier Background IP solely as embedded in or necessary for use of the deliverables for Customer's internal business purposes.
rows.reason: Customer's license to Supplier Background IP is contingent on full payment and is non-transferable. This creates operational risk if the engagement terminates or if Customer needs to transfer operations to another provider. The contract does not clarify whether the license survives termination or what happens to embedded IP in deliverables.

rows.measurement: Not applicable
rows.clause: Security operations center, managed detection and response, penetration testing, vulnerability scanning ownership, or security incident response ownership are out of scope.
rows.valueAtRisk: Customer must provide or procure separate security operations, detection, response, and vulnerability management services; potential security gaps if not addressed
rows.customerDependency: Customer must ensure security operations, detection, response, penetration testing, and vulnerability scanning are provided by Customer or third-party vendors
rows.exclusions: Supplier not responsible for security operations center, managed detection and response, penetration testing, vulnerability scanning ownership, or security incident response ownership
rows.recommendedAction: Validate that Customer has adequate security operations, detection, response, and vulnerability management capabilities in place. Clarify Supplier's role in supporting security incidents (e.g., coordination, evidence collection, remediation support). Consider adding basic security monitoring or incident coordination to Supplier's scope. Ensure third-party security vendors have clear SLAs and integration with Supplier's ITSM processes.
rows.evidence: Security operations center, managed detection and response, penetration testing, vulnerability scanning ownership, or security incident response ownership are out of scope.
rows.reason: Security operations, detection, response, and vulnerability management are critical for infrastructure services but are explicitly excluded from Supplier's scope. Customer must ensure these capabilities are provided by Customer or third-party vendors. Gaps in security coverage could result in undetected or unresolved security incidents.

rows.measurement: Not applicable
rows.clause: Database administration beyond basic service availability checks unless separately contracted.
rows.valueAtRisk: Customer must provide or procure separate database administration services; potential database performance, availability, or security issues if not addressed
rows.customerDependency: Customer must ensure database administration is provided by Customer or third-party vendors
rows.exclusions: Supplier not responsible for database administration beyond basic service availability checks
rows.recommendedAction: Validate that Customer has adequate database administration capabilities in place. Clarify what 'basic service availability checks' includes (e.g., database service status, connectivity checks). Consider adding database administration to Supplier's scope if Customer lacks internal DBA resources. Ensure third-party DBA vendors have clear SLAs and integration with Supplier's ITSM processes.
rows.evidence: Database administration beyond basic service availability checks unless separately contracted.
rows.reason: Database administration is critical for application and infrastructure services but is largely excluded from Supplier's scope. Customer must ensure database administration is provided by Customer or third-party vendors. Gaps in DBA coverage could result in database performance, availability, or security issues.

rows.deliverable: Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Exclusion
rows.measurement: Not applicable
rows.clause: Ownership of Customer business continuity, disaster recovery strategy, regulatory compliance, or final operational risk decisions are out of scope.
rows.valueAtRisk: Customer retains ownership and accountability for business continuity, disaster recovery, regulatory compliance, and operational risk decisions; potential business impact if Customer lacks adequate BC/DR capabilities
rows.customerDependency: Customer must own and maintain business continuity, disaster recovery strategy, regulatory compliance, and operational risk decisions
rows.exclusions: Supplier not responsible for ownership of Customer business continuity, disaster recovery strategy, regulatory compliance, or final operational risk decisions
rows.recommendedAction: Validate that Customer has adequate business continuity and disaster recovery plans, capabilities, and testing in place. Clarify Supplier's role in supporting BC/DR activities (e.g., backup monitoring, restore coordination, DR drill support). Consider adding BC/DR coordination or support to Supplier's scope. Ensure Customer's BC/DR plans align with Supplier's SLA commitments and service delivery model.
rows.evidence: Ownership of Customer business continuity, disaster recovery strategy, regulatory compliance, or final operational risk decisions are out of scope.
rows.reason: Business continuity, disaster recovery, regulatory compliance, and operational risk decisions are critical for infrastructure services but are explicitly excluded from Supplier's ownership. Customer retains full accountability. Gaps in BC/DR capabilities could result in extended outages or data loss during disasters.

rows.measurement: Not applicable
rows.clause: Supplier shall be entitled to payment for all Services performed up to the effective date of termination, including accepted Services, work in progress, and any investments or third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of Customer.
rows.valueAtRisk: Customer must pay for all Services, work in progress, and third-party costs incurred up to termination date, even if terminating for cause or convenience
rows.customerDependency: Customer must pay Supplier for all Services performed up to termination date
rows.exclusions: Not stated in uploaded contract
rows.recommendedAction: Negotiate that payment entitlement upon termination depends on the termination reason (e.g., no payment for work in progress if terminating for Supplier cause or material breach). Clarify what constitutes 'work in progress' and 'investments or third-party costs' and require supporting documentation. Negotiate a cap on termination payments or require Customer approval for third-party costs. Ensure termination provisions in the MSA align with this SOW.
rows.evidence: Supplier shall be entitled to payment for all Services performed up to the effective date of termination, including accepted Services, work in progress, and any investments or third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of Customer.
rows.reason: Supplier is entitled to payment for all Services, work in progress, and third-party costs up to termination date, regardless of termination reason. This could result in significant termination costs for Customer, especially if terminating for Supplier cause or material breach. The contract does not define 'work in progress' or 'investments' or require supporting documentation.

rows.measurement: Not applicable
rows.clause: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties set forth below have executed this SOW as of the SOW Effective Date. Signature blocks present for both parties but signature lines are blank/unfilled in the uploaded document.
rows.valueAtRisk: Contract may not be legally binding if not properly executed by authorized signatories; potential disputes over contract validity, enforceability, or effective date
rows.customerDependency: Customer must ensure SOW is executed by authorized signatories with proper authority
rows.exclusions: Not applicable
rows.recommendedAction: Obtain fully executed copy of SOW with authorized signatures, names, titles, and dates for both parties. Validate that signatories have proper authority to bind their respective organizations. Confirm effective date and ensure it aligns with service start date and billing commencement. Maintain executed copy in contract repository.
rows.evidence: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties set forth below have executed this SOW as of the SOW Effective Date.
rows.reason: The uploaded document shows blank signature lines, indicating the SOW may not be fully executed. Without proper execution by authorized signatories, the contract may not be legally binding or enforceable. This creates risk around contract validity, obligations, and effective date.

rows.section: Annexure 1 - Service Transition and Delivery Plan
rows.target: Kick-off by 15-Jun-26, Monitoring readiness by 31-Jun-26, Steady-state by 01-Sep-26
rows.measurement: Milestone completion dates and acceptance outputs
rows.clause: The following plan is indicative and may be refined during transition based on Customer infrastructure readiness, access availability, and server inventory finalization. Milestone 1: Kick-off by 15-Jun-26. Milestone 2: Monitoring readiness by 31-Jun-26. Milestone 3: Steady-state by 01-Sep-26.
rows.valueAtRisk: Delays in service transition and steady-state operations; potential impact on SLA commencement and service delivery; no penalties for milestone delays
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide infrastructure readiness, access availability, and server inventory finalization to support transition milestones
rows.exclusions: Transition plan is indicative and may be refined based on Customer readiness
rows.owner: Supplier Transition Manager / Customer Service Owner
rows.recommendedAction: Negotiate firm milestone dates with clear acceptance criteria and accountability for delays. Clarify whether SLA commitments commence on 01-Jun-26 (SOW effective date) or 01-Sep-26 (steady-state start). Establish clear escalation process and remedies if milestones are delayed due to Supplier or Customer issues. Require weekly transition status reporting and risk tracking. Consider adding milestone-based payment holdbacks or incentives.
rows.evidence: The following plan is indicative and may be refined during transition based on Customer infrastructure readiness, access availability, and server inventory finalization.
rows.reason: Transition milestones are 'indicative' and may be refined, creating uncertainty around service start dates and SLA commencement. The contract does not specify accountability or remedies for milestone delays. Customer may be paying monthly fees (starting 01-Jun-26) before steady-state operations begin (01-Sep-26), creating a 3-month gap.

rows.measurement: Not applicable
rows.clause: The commercial model below is sample commercial wording and may be revised by the parties before execution.
rows.valueAtRisk: Commercial terms (monthly fee, annual value, billing frequency, payment terms) are sample only and may be revised; potential for disputes or renegotiation before execution
rows.customerDependency: Customer must finalize and agree commercial terms before execution
rows.exclusions: Not applicable
rows.recommendedAction: Finalize and confirm all commercial terms (monthly fee, annual value, billing frequency, payment terms, taxes, invoice dispute window) before execution. Remove 'sample commercial wording' language and replace with final agreed terms. Ensure commercial terms are consistent with budget approval and procurement authority. Obtain written confirmation from both parties that commercial terms are final.
rows.evidence: The commercial model below is sample commercial wording and may be revised by the parties before execution.
rows.reason: The contract explicitly states that commercial terms are 'sample' and may be revised before execution. This creates uncertainty around the final monthly fee (USD 25,000), annual value (USD 300,000), and other payment terms. Customer should not execute the SOW until commercial terms are finalized and confirmed.

boardSummary.statement: SOW governed by unuploaded MSA dated 01-Jun-26; material terms unknown and require validation.
SLA remedy capped at 5% monthly and 10% annual; low relative to contract value and business impact.
4-hour P1 resolution target with broad exclusions and coordination-only accountability for third-party dependencies.
Extensive Customer obligations for access, tools, approvals, and third-party support; delays trigger SLA exclusions.
Signature blocks blank; commercial terms labeled sample; execution evidence and finalization required before start.

proceedIf.statement: Obtain and review Master Services Agreement dated 01-Jun-26 to validate liability caps, indemnity, termination rights, IP ownership, payment terms, and dispute resolution before accepting this SOW.
Confirm and finalize all commercial terms (monthly fee USD 25,000, annual value USD 300,000, billing, payment terms) and remove 'sample commercial wording' language before execution.
Obtain fully executed SOW with authorized signatures, names, titles, and dates for both parties; validate signatory authority and confirm effective date.
Validate Customer's ability to provide required access, tools, licenses, server inventory, approvals, and third-party support per transition plan milestones by 15-Jun-26.
Clarify whether SLA commitments commence on 01-Jun-26 (effective date) or 01-Sep-26 (steady-state start) and align billing with service delivery readiness.

renegotiate.statement: Increase service credit percentages (10%-20% for severe failures) and remove or raise cumulative cap (currently 10% annual, USD 30,000) to provide meaningful SLA enforcement.
Tighten P1 resolution accountability and reduce exclusions; require Supplier to prove Customer/third-party causation; add termination rights for repeated P1 failures.
Define specific Customer approval turnaround commitments (e.g., P1 approvals within 30 minutes); clarify consequences if Customer fails to meet dependencies.
Extend monthly service report acceptance period (15-20 business days) and invoice dispute window (30 business days) to allow adequate Customer review time.
Negotiate perpetual, irrevocable license to Supplier Background IP embedded in deliverables; add rights to transfer or sublicense to affiliates or successors.

financialNotes.statement: USD 300,000 Annual Contract Value for 12-month initial term (01-Jun-26 to 31-May-27).
USD 25,000 fixed monthly fee billed monthly in arrears; payment terms Net 30 days from invoice date.
Commercial terms labeled 'sample commercial wording and may be revised by the parties before execution' — finalization required.
Maximum service credit: 5% monthly (USD 1,250) and 10% cumulative annual (USD 30,000) for SLA failures; low relative to contract value.
Additional servers, upgrades, migrations, DR, security projects, and onsite support handled as Time & Material or Change Request — pricing not stated.
Supplier entitled to payment for all Services, work in progress, and third-party costs up to termination date regardless of termination reason.

revenueControls.title: Monthly Service Report Acceptance
revenueControls.detail: Services deemed accepted if Customer does not raise written non-conformance within 10 business days of monthly report receipt. Customer must review reports promptly to preserve dispute and service credit rights.

revenueControls.title: Invoice Dispute Window
revenueControls.detail: Invoices deemed accepted if disputed items not raised within 15 business days. Customer must review invoices promptly to dispute incorrect charges or claim service credits.

revenueControls.title: Service Credit Claims
revenueControls.detail: Customer must track SLA performance monthly and raise service credit claims within invoice dispute window (15 business days). Service credits applied against future invoices, not cash refund.

revenueControls.title: Change Request Approval
revenueControls.detail: All scope, server count, support hours, tooling, SLA, or fee changes require written change request or amendment signed by both parties. Customer must control change approval process to prevent scope creep.

revenueControls.title: Server Baseline Monitoring
revenueControls.detail: Services based on maximum 150 servers. Customer must monitor server count and request change request before exceeding baseline to avoid service delivery issues or unplanned fees.

commercialBaseline.interpretation: Fixed monthly fee covering 24x7 monitoring, server administration, incident/problem/change management, service desk, reporting, and governance for up to 150 servers. Labeled 'sample commercial wording' — finalization required.
Total contract value for 12-month initial term (01-Jun-26 to 31-May-27). Based on USD 25,000 monthly fee × 12 months.
Supplier invoices monthly after Services are performed. Customer pays Net 30 days from invoice date.
Customer must pay invoices within 30 days of invoice date. Late payment terms not stated in uploaded contract.
Maximum service credit in any calendar month for SLA failures. Low relative to monthly fee and potential business impact.
Maximum cumulative service credits during 12-month initial term. Low relative to contract value and potential business impact of repeated SLA failures.
Services based on maximum 150 servers. Additional servers require change request or revised monthly fee. Pricing model for additional servers not stated.
Monthly fee excludes taxes. Customer responsible for applicable taxes (e.g., GST, VAT, sales tax).

leakageScenarios.scenario: SLA Failure Without Service Credit Claim
leakageScenarios.impact: Customer fails to track SLA performance or raise service credit claims within invoice dispute window (15 business days). Invoices deemed accepted; service credit rights lost. Potential leakage: USD 1,250 per month (5% service credit).
leakageScenarios.control: Implement monthly SLA performance tracking and review process. Designate owner to review monthly service reports within 10 business days and raise service credit claims within invoice dispute window.
leakageScenarios.evidence: Monthly service report with SLA dashboard; invoice dispute documentation; service credit claim records.

leakageScenarios.scenario: Server Count Exceeds Baseline Without Change Request
leakageScenarios.impact: Customer adds servers beyond 150-server baseline without requesting change request or revised monthly fee. Supplier may claim additional fees retroactively or reduce service quality. Potential leakage: unplanned fees or service delivery issues.
leakageScenarios.control: Implement server inventory monitoring and change control process. Designate owner to track server count monthly and request change request before exceeding baseline. Negotiate per-server pricing model upfront.
leakageScenarios.evidence: Server inventory reports; change request documentation; revised fee agreements.

leakageScenarios.impact: Customer fails to review monthly service reports and raise written non-conformance within 10 business days. Services deemed accepted; dispute rights lost. Potential leakage: payment for non-conforming Services or inability to claim service credits.
leakageScenarios.control: Implement monthly service report review process with clear accountability and deadlines. Designate owner to review reports within 10 business days and raise non-conformance with supporting evidence.
leakageScenarios.evidence: Monthly service report review records; non-conformance documentation; acceptance/rejection records.

leakageScenarios.impact: Customer approves change requests for additional servers, upgrades, migrations, DR, security projects, or onsite support without validating pricing or budget impact. Potential leakage: unplanned fees or budget overruns.
leakageScenarios.control: Implement change request approval process with pricing validation and budget approval. Require detailed cost estimates and business justification for all change requests. Negotiate Time & Material rates upfront.
leakageScenarios.evidence: Change request documentation; cost estimates; budget approval records; Time & Material rate agreements.

leakageScenarios.impact: Customer terminates SOW and pays Supplier for all Services, work in progress, and third-party costs without validating supporting documentation. Potential leakage: payment for incomplete or non-performed Services or inflated third-party costs.
leakageScenarios.control: Negotiate termination payment terms that depend on termination reason. Require detailed supporting documentation for work in progress and third-party costs. Validate all termination invoices before payment.
leakageScenarios.evidence: Termination notice; work in progress documentation; third-party cost invoices; payment validation records.

penaltyExposure.High: P1 Critical Incident Resolution (4-hour target): Service credit exposure USD 500 - USD 1,250 per month (2%-5% of monthly fee) if monthly SLA achievement falls below 98%. Maximum cumulative exposure USD 30,000 (10% of annual value). Broad exclusions for Customer, third-party, tool, approval, maintenance, and force majeure issues significantly limit Customer's ability to claim service credits. Potential business impact from extended P1 outages not covered by service credits.
penaltyExposure.Medium: P2 High Incident Resolution (8-hour target), P1/P2 Response Times (15/30 minutes): Service credit exposure USD 500 - USD 1,250 per month (2%-5% of monthly fee) if monthly SLA achievement falls below 98%. Maximum cumulative exposure USD 30,000 (10% of annual value). Broad exclusions and 'coordination only' language for third-party dependencies limit Supplier's accountability and Customer's remedy.
penaltyExposure.Low: P3 Medium and P4 Low Incident/Service Request Response and Resolution: Service credit exposure USD 500 - USD 1,250 per month (2%-5% of monthly fee) if monthly SLA achievement falls below 98%. Maximum cumulative exposure USD 30,000 (10% of annual value). Broad exclusions limit Customer's ability to claim service credits for P3/P4 SLA failures.

recos.action: Negotiate higher service credit percentages and remove or raise cumulative cap to provide meaningful SLA enforcement and financial remedy for repeated failures.
recos.original: Maximum service credit in any calendar month shall not exceed five percent (5%) of the Monthly Managed Services Fee. Maximum cumulative service credits during the initial term shall not exceed ten percent (10%) of the Annual Contract Value.
recos.rewrite: Maximum service credit in any calendar month shall not exceed twenty percent (20%) of the Monthly Managed Services Fee. Maximum cumulative service credits during the initial term shall not exceed thirty percent (30%) of the Annual Contract Value. If monthly SLA achievement falls below 90% for two consecutive months, Customer shall have the right to terminate this SOW for cause with 30 days' notice without termination fees.

recos.action: Tighten P1 resolution accountability, reduce exclusions, and add termination rights for repeated P1 SLA failures to ensure Supplier is accountable for critical incident resolution.
recos.original: Resolution targets assume that Supplier has required access, approvals, information, and third-party/vendor support availability. Where final resolution depends on Customer, third-party vendors, hardware replacement, telecom/network provider, cloud provider, or application vendor, Supplier shall be responsible for coordination and commercially reasonable support, not guaranteed resolution.
recos.rewrite: Supplier shall be accountable for P1 Critical Incident resolution within 4 hours, including coordination and escalation of third-party vendors, hardware replacement, telecom/network providers, cloud providers, and application vendors. Supplier shall maintain vendor support agreements and escalation paths to meet P1 resolution targets. Service credit exclusions for third-party dependencies shall apply only if Supplier can demonstrate that (a) Supplier escalated the issue to the third party within 30 minutes of incident logging, (b) the third party failed to respond or resolve within their committed SLA, and (c) Supplier provided all necessary information and support to the third party. If P1 resolution SLA achievement falls below 90% for two consecutive months, Customer shall have the right to terminate this SOW for cause with 30 days' notice without termination fees.

recos.action: Obtain and review the Master Services Agreement dated 01-Jun-26 before accepting this SOW to validate liability caps, indemnity, termination rights, IP ownership, payment terms, and dispute resolution.
recos.original: This SOW shall be governed by the terms and conditions contained in the Master Services Agreement dated 01-Jun-26. In case of conflict between this SOW and the Agreement, the Agreement shall prevail unless expressly stated otherwise in this SOW.
recos.rewrite: This SOW shall be governed by the terms and conditions contained in the Master Services Agreement dated 01-Jun-26, a copy of which is attached as Exhibit A. In case of conflict between this SOW and the Agreement, the terms of this SOW shall prevail with respect to the specific Services, SLAs, service credits, and commercial terms described herein. The Agreement shall prevail with respect to general terms such as liability caps, indemnity, confidentiality, IP ownership, termination rights, payment terms, and dispute resolution, unless expressly modified in this SOW.

recos.action: Define specific Customer approval turnaround time commitments and clarify consequences if Customer fails to meet dependencies to ensure balanced accountability.
recos.original: Provide timely approvals for changes, access, emergency workarounds, downtime, and remediation actions.
recos.rewrite: Customer shall provide approvals for changes, access, emergency workarounds, downtime, and remediation actions within the following timeframes: (a) P1 Critical approvals within 30 minutes of Supplier's request, (b) P2 High approvals within 2 hours, (c) P3 Medium approvals within 4 business hours, (d) P4 Low approvals within 1 business day. If Customer fails to provide approvals within these timeframes, Supplier's SLA obligations shall be suspended for the duration of the approval delay, and the incident/service request clock shall be paused. Supplier shall document all approval requests and Customer response times in the ticketing system.

recos.contract: Section 16 - Monthly Service Report Acceptance Period
recos.action: Extend monthly service report acceptance period to allow adequate Customer review time and clarify that deemed acceptance does not waive service credit rights.
recos.original: Services are deemed accepted monthly if Supplier provides the monthly service report and Customer does not raise written material non-conformance within ten (10) business days.
recos.rewrite: Services are deemed accepted monthly if Supplier provides the monthly service report and Customer does not raise written material non-conformance within twenty (20) business days from receipt of the report. Deemed acceptance of the monthly service report shall not waive Customer's right to claim service credits for SLA failures documented in the report, provided such service credit claims are raised within the invoice dispute window (30 business days from invoice date).

recos.action: Extend invoice dispute window to allow adequate Customer review time and clarify that deemed acceptance does not waive service credit rights.
recos.original: Invoices deemed accepted if disputed items are not raised within 15 business days.
recos.rewrite: Invoices deemed accepted if disputed items are not raised within thirty (30) business days from invoice date. Deemed acceptance of invoices shall not waive Customer's right to claim service credits for SLA failures documented in the monthly service report, provided such service credit claims are raised within the invoice dispute window.

recos.action: Negotiate perpetual, irrevocable license to Supplier Background IP embedded in deliverables and add rights to transfer or sublicense to affiliates or successors.
recos.original: Subject to full payment of applicable fees, Supplier grants Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable right to use such Supplier Background IP solely as embedded in or necessary for use of the deliverables for Customer's internal business purposes.
recos.rewrite: Subject to full payment of applicable fees, Supplier grants Customer a perpetual, irrevocable, non-exclusive, worldwide right and license to use, reproduce, modify, and create derivative works of Supplier Background IP solely as embedded in or necessary for use of the deliverables for Customer's internal business purposes. Customer may transfer or sublicense such rights to Customer's affiliates, successors, assigns, and outsourcing providers solely for Customer's internal business purposes. Upon termination of this SOW, Customer's license to Supplier Background IP embedded in deliverables shall survive and continue in perpetuity.

recos.action: Negotiate higher server baseline or tiered pricing model to accommodate growth and establish clear per-server pricing for additional servers.
recos.original: Services are based on a maximum baseline of 150 servers unless otherwise agreed in writing.
recos.rewrite: Services are based on a maximum baseline of 200 servers. If Customer's server count exceeds 200 servers, Supplier shall provide support for additional servers at a rate of USD 150 per server per month, subject to written change request approval. Supplier shall notify Customer when server count reaches 180 servers (90% of baseline) to allow proactive planning for additional capacity.

recos.action: Negotiate that payment entitlement upon termination depends on termination reason and require supporting documentation for work in progress and third-party costs.
recos.original: Supplier shall be entitled to payment for all Services performed up to the effective date of termination, including accepted Services, work in progress, and any investments or third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of Customer.
recos.rewrite: Upon termination of this SOW: (a) If Customer terminates for convenience or Supplier terminates for Customer cause, Supplier shall be entitled to payment for all accepted Services performed up to the effective date of termination, plus reasonable wind-down costs and documented third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of Customer. (b) If Customer terminates for Supplier cause or material breach, Supplier shall be entitled to payment only for accepted Services performed up to the effective date of termination; Supplier shall not be entitled to payment for work in progress, wind-down costs, or third-party costs. (c) Supplier shall provide detailed supporting documentation for all work in progress, wind-down costs, and third-party costs within 15 business days of termination.

recos.action: Negotiate firm milestone dates with clear acceptance criteria and accountability for delays, and clarify SLA commencement date.
recos.original: The following plan is indicative and may be refined during transition based on Customer infrastructure readiness, access availability, and server inventory finalization.
recos.rewrite: The following transition plan milestones are firm commitments subject to Customer providing infrastructure readiness, access availability, and server inventory finalization per the agreed transition checklist. Milestone 1: Kick-off and transition plan approval by 15-Jun-26. Milestone 2: Monitoring and ticket workflow readiness by 30-Jun-26. Milestone 3: Steady-state operations start by 01-Aug-26. SLA commitments (response and resolution targets) shall commence on 01-Aug-26 (steady-state start date). If milestones are delayed due to Supplier issues, Supplier shall provide weekly status reports and corrective action plans. If milestones are delayed due to Customer issues, the milestone dates and SLA commencement date shall be extended by the duration of the Customer-caused delay.

assumptions.assumption: This SOW is governed by the Master Services Agreement dated 01-Jun-26, which is referenced but not uploaded. Material commercial, legal, liability, indemnity, termination, IP, payment, and dispute resolution terms in the MSA will control this engagement. Without reviewing the MSA, the full risk profile, liability exposure, termination rights, and unfavorable terms cannot be assessed. Customer must obtain and review the MSA before accepting this SOW.

Annual Contract Value of USD 300,000 is based on USD 25,000 monthly fee × 12 months for the initial term (01-Jun-26 to 31-May-27). The contract explicitly states commercial terms are 'sample commercial wording and may be revised by the parties before execution,' so the USD 300,000 value is not final until execution. Contract Value at Risk allocation uses High 50% (USD 150,000), Medium 40% (USD 120,000), Low 10% (USD 30,000) based on risk row counts (2 High, 18 Medium, 3 Low) as a directional model, since the contract does not provide a basis for allocating value at risk by risk level.

Service credit exposure is limited to 5% of Monthly Managed Services Fee per month (USD 1,250) and 10% of Annual Contract Value cumulative (USD 30,000). These caps are explicitly stated in Section 13. Broad exclusions for Customer, third-party, tool, approval, maintenance, and force majeure issues significantly limit Customer's ability to claim service credits. Potential business impact from SLA failures (e.g., extended P1 outages, lost revenue, customer dissatisfaction) is not covered by service credits and is excluded per the 'sole and exclusive remedy' language.

EM level is classified as EM3 – Delivery+ based on the following evidence: (1) Supplier owns delivery scheduling, milestone tracking, and meeting milestones per the Service Transition and Delivery Plan (Annexure 1). (2) Supplier has SLA/KPI commitments (response and resolution targets for P1/P2/P3/P4) with service credit remedies. (3) However, the commercial model is fixed monthly fee (not fixed-price project), service credit remedies are lenient with caps and broad exclusions (not true penalties), and scope changes are handled through change requests rather than supplier-owned scope management. (4) The contract does not indicate fixed-price budget ownership, output-based pricing, or outcome-based pricing. Therefore, EM3 is the most appropriate classification. This is NOT EM4 because the pricing model is fixed monthly fee (not fixed-price project), service credits are capped and have broad exclusions (not true penalties), and Supplier does not own budget or scope (changes require change requests).

Pricing model is 'Fixed Monthly Fee' (managed services model) and is consistent with EM3 classification. This is NOT a fixed-price project (EM4 – Fixed Price) because the engagement is ongoing managed services with a fixed monthly fee, not a one-time deliverable-based project with a fixed total price. This is NOT output-based or outcome-based pricing (EM4 – Output+ or EM4 – Outcome+) because the contract does not contain output metrics, velocity commitments, or business outcome dependencies. The monthly fee is fixed regardless of output or outcome.

SLA measurement methodology (e.g., how response time and resolution time are measured, what constitutes 'initial response' vs. 'technical engagement,' how monthly SLA achievement percentage is calculated) is not explicitly defined in the uploaded contract. The contract states response and resolution targets but does not specify measurement methodology, reporting frequency, or calculation formulas. Customer should clarify measurement methodology and ensure it is documented in the monthly service report format before accepting this SOW.

Business hours, business days, and holidays are not explicitly defined in the uploaded contract. P3 and P4 SLA targets reference 'business hours' and 'business days,' but the contract does not specify time zone, days of the week, or holiday calendar. Customer should clarify business hours definition and ensure it aligns with Customer's operational expectations and Supplier's support coverage.

Page numbers are derived from [[PAGE n]] markers in the uploaded PDF text. Each ledger row's pageNo field is set to the number from the nearest preceding [[PAGE n]] marker above the clause. Page numbers are reliable for this uploaded document. Evidence quotes are verbatim excerpts from the contract text to enable the application to re-derive page numbers by locating the evidence quote in the source text.

Confidence scores reflect the strength and clarity of evidence in the uploaded contract. High confidence (0.9-0.95) indicates clear, explicit contract language. Medium confidence (0.85-0.9) indicates reasonable inference from contract language but some ambiguity or dependency on unuploaded agreements. Lower confidence would indicate weak evidence or significant ambiguity, but all rows in this analysis have medium to high confidence based on explicit contract language.

The uploaded document shows signature blocks for both parties (Yen Corp. Ltd. and CTIND Pvt. Ltd.) but the signature lines are blank/unfilled. This indicates the SOW may not be fully executed. Without proper execution by authorized signatories, the contract may not be legally binding or enforceable. Customer must obtain a fully executed copy with authorized signatures, names, titles, and dates before treating this SOW as binding.

The Master Services Agreement dated 01-Jun-26 is referenced throughout the SOW but is not uploaded. Material terms in the MSA (liability caps, indemnity, termination rights, IP ownership, payment terms, dispute resolution, governing law) will control this engagement and may override or supplement terms in this SOW. Without reviewing the MSA, the full risk profile cannot be assessed. All assumptions and recommendations in this analysis are subject to validation against the MSA terms.

The uploaded contract text appears to be clean and well-formatted, suggesting it was extracted from a digital PDF or DOCX source rather than OCR. No obvious OCR errors or garbled text were observed. However, Customer should manually validate all critical values (parties, dates, monetary amounts, SLA targets, service credit percentages, caps) against the original signed contract to ensure accuracy.
